name: se7enxweb/sevenx_themes_admin_classic
description: sevenx_themes_admin_classic is a compatibility-first, operator-friendly eZ Publish Legacy / Exponential Platform Legacy extension that introduces a controlled Admin Design switch workflow, session-aware visual behavior toggling, and classic admin ergonomics for teams that maintain long-lived editorial back offices and need predictable, reversible, low-risk UI transitions.

suggest.ext-json: Improves portability and resilience for metadata-driven integrations and diagnostic tooling in legacy deployments.
suggest.ext-mbstring: Recommended for robust multibyte handling in editorial environments that publish multilingual content.
suggest.ext-iconv: Recommended where character set normalization and safe legacy encoding conversions are expected.
suggest.ext-session: Expected for session-based admin design mode switching behavior.

sevenx-meta.project-intent: Provide an intentionally explicit and reversible admin design switch capability that keeps SiteDesign stable while allowing AdditionalSiteDesignList behavior to adapt by session context.
sevenx-meta.operational-philosophy: Bias toward predictable behavior, transparent configuration layering, and minimal-kernel-touch architecture suitable for conservative production stacks.

configuration-contract.guideline: Environment-specific values belong in settings/override; extension-level settings remain portable defaults.

feature-map: Admin Design switch endpoint with explicit mode handling
High-limit subitems pagination controls with safe defaults
runtime-assumptions: Legacy template resolver behavior may cache design location data per siteaccess
Session persistence is available and enabled
Administrative users can access user preference routes
safety-notes: Keep extension defaults generic to avoid environment lock-in
Use override files for deployment-specific siteaccess identifiers
Clear template cache after toolbar/template modifications

maintainability-notes.change-management: Prefer additive, isolated changes and avoid global behavior regressions
